danger_signals.visual_form: Animated enemy sprites filling screen space during scrolling segments
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat threat requiring evasion and weapon discharge

danger_signals.visual_form: Large-scale guardian sprite at end of side-view stages, described as 'huge end-of-level guardians'
danger_signals.meaning: Significant difficulty spike; stage completion gate

danger_signals.visual_form: Ship destruction animation; loss of equipped weapons except defaults
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability state; player reverts to basic Twin/Back shot and bombs

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen 'packed to the gills with enemy ships' in side-view stages
danger_signals.meaning: High incoming fire density; need for evasion and CRAW pod blocking

danger_signals.visual_form: Loss of orbiting pod add-ons upon ship destruction
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of bullet-blocking capability and supplementary firepower

safety_signals.visual_form: Orbiting pod items collected; up to two revolving around player ship
safety_signals.meaning: Defensive reinforcement; passive bullet blocking and extra firepower

safety_signals.visual_form: Item pickup resulting in weapon power-level increase or new weapon acquisition
safety_signals.meaning: Offensive capability enhancement; access to signature weapons like Hunter

safety_signals.visual_form: Temporary item increasing orbit speed of pods
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced bullet-blocking probability; defensive advantage window

safety_signals.visual_form: Transition from top-view base destruction to side-view horizontal scroll
safety_signals.meaning: Mission objective achieved; game state advancement

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: FIRE LEO series fighter craft designation (player control asset)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Controlled territory undergoing ORN assault; planets Reda and Nepura/Nebula

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Plealos battleship (primary strategic asset); enemy ships and bases
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: All hostile enemy sprites encountered in both top-view and side-view stages represent ORN military forces
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Occupied planets; underground Plealos housing facility on Nepura; scattered military bases requiring destruction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dense enemy presence throughout stages; multiple enemy bases indicated in top-view segments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous enemy fire and ship spawning; bases must be actively located and destroyed by player

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage environments represent occupied territory; Plealos battleship functions as stage goal
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must destroy ORN bases and penetrate defended airspace to reach final battleship target

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player ship (FIRE LEO-02 Exceliza) represents Federation military response; mission objective to eliminate Plealos
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multi-stage progression structure: top-view base targeting followed by side-view stage completion with boss defeat

environmental_storytelling.example: Top-view segments represent strategic reconnaissance and base targeting phase; transition to side-view represents tactical combat engagement phase
Plealos battleship serves as both mechanical enemy and symbol of ORN Empire's military power; its destruction is final narrative goal
Reda's destruction and Nepura's heavy damage communicate ORN's strategic success; player mission to recapture Nepura and eliminate Plealos demonstrates Federation recovery attempt
Multiple ORN bases scattered across stages imply occupation depth and defensive infrastructure; required destruction conveys liberation narrative

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Continuous spawning and firing in patterns throughout scrolling stages; dense field indicates coordinated defensive presence
Twin forward shot, single forward/backward shot, bomb capability; baseline engagement capability
Increased offensive output through collected weapons (Hunter etc.); passive defense through orbiting pods blocking incoming fire
Large-scale sprite with high health; represents major ORN defensive asset; defeat required for stage completion
Automatic bullet interception and supplementary fire output; lost upon ship destruction, requiring re-acquisition

notes: Thunder Force II communicates faction conflict through binary spatial organization: top-view stages represent strategic theater (locating and destroying ORN infrastructure), while side-view stages represent tactical engagement (surviving enemy fire and defeating stage bosses). The Plealos battleship functions as both mechanical enemy and political symbol—its location beneath Nebula's surface and eventual destruction frame the entire narrative arc. Visual progression from reconnaissance (top-view) to assault (side-view) mirrors military operational doctrine. Enemy density and boss scale directly signal danger escalation. Weapon and CRAW systems provide visual feedback on defensive/offensive capability states. No faction color differentiation noted in available sources; faction identity relies on context (player-controlled vs. hostile) rather than palette.
